2604.12994.title: LogicEval: A Systematic Framework for Evaluating Automated Repair Techniques for Logical Vulnerabilities in Real-World Software
2604.12994.paper_summary: Logical vulnerabilities in software stem from flaws in program logic rather than memory safety, which can lead to critical security failures. Although existing automated program repair techniques primarily focus on repairing memory corruption vulnerabilities, they struggle with logical vulnerabilities because of their limited semantic understanding of the vulnerable code and its expected behavior. On the other hand, recent successes of large language models (LLMs) in understanding and repairing code are promising. However, no framework currently exists to analyze the capabilities and limitations of such techniques for logical vulnerabilities. This paper aims to systematically evaluate both traditional and LLM-based repair approaches for addressing real-world logical vulnerabilities. To facilitate our assessment, we created the first ever dataset, LogicDS, of 86 logical vulnerabilities with assigned CVEs reflecting tangible security impact. We also developed a systematic framework, LogicEval, to evaluate patches for logical vulnerabilities. Evaluations suggest that compilation and testing failures are primarily driven by prompt sensitivity, loss of code context, and difficulty in patch localization.
2604.12994.paper_summary_zh: 邏輯漏洞在軟體中源於程式邏輯的缺陷，而非記憶體安全，這可能導致關鍵的安全失敗。儘管現有的自動程式修復技術主要集中在修復記憶體損壞漏洞，但因為對脆弱代碼及其預期行為的語義理解有限，它們在處理邏輯漏洞時面臨困難。另一方面，最近大型語言模型（LLMs）在理解和修復代碼方面的成功令人鼓舞。然而，目前尚不存在分析這些技術在邏輯漏洞方面的能力和限制的框架。本文旨在系統性地評估傳統和基於LLM的修復方法，以應對現實世界中的邏輯漏洞。為了促進我們的評估，我們創建了首個數據集LogicDS，包含86個邏輯漏洞，並分配了反映實際安全影響的CVE。我們還開發了一個系統化框架LogicEval，用於評估邏輯漏洞的修補程式。評估結果表明，編譯和測試失敗主要受到提示敏感性、代碼上下文丟失以及修補定位困難的驅動。
2604.12994.author: Syed Md Mukit Rashid et.al.
2604.12994.authors: Syed Md Mukit Rashid, Abdullah Al Ishtiaq, Kai Tu, Yilu Dong, Tianwei Wu, Ali Ranjbar, Tianchang Yang, Najrin Sultana, Shagufta Mehnaz, Syed Rafiul Hussain
